meaning: adj.大钉似的,长而尖的,装有大钉的,尖刻的
examples: The two firms are bombarding one another in barbed letters and spiky press conferences.
It's interesting to see that Jobs, who has a notoriously spiky attitude towards things past, is very fuzzy in the early days of Apple.
Her short spiky hair is damp with sweat.
Like many top scrum-halves Dawson has a spiky side to his character, as he freely admits.
Spiky heels, plastic frames and hair neatly combed back into long, straight ponytails polished the look.
